subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + independent/international press (Iraqi Oil Report, Rudaw, Al-Monitor) + HRW/Amnesty; Iraqi official data used cautiously.

A clearly-negative oil rentier whose standout drags are a severe water/heat crisis, electricity failure amid oil wealth, militia-captured institutions, and a fragile sectarian state caught in the US-Iran contest.
Opacity around militia/PMF activity and Kurdistan-internal politics inflates uncertainty and caps confidence. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone/multilateral.

economic.notes: A catastrophic single-commodity oil rentier with a managed dinar, a weak banking sector, and near-zero diversification.

fiscal_state.notes: About 90% of revenue from oil, a bloated public payroll, and deficits when crude dips. (IMF Art IV; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: An oil-dollar-funded managed dinar quasi-peg, with dollar-auction reforms but persistent dollar-access frictions and a parallel-rate gap. (IMF; Iraqi Oil Report)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dinar has no international role. (definitional)

banking_stability.notes: Weak and heavily cash-based, with correspondent-banking and sanctioned-bank exclusions. (IMF; US Treasury)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Vast reserves as an OPEC member but a catastrophic single-commodity rentier with near-zero diversification, chronic gas flaring, and Iran energy dependence. (Iraqi Oil Report; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Pervasive corruption, militia influence, weak courts, and a poor press environment.

rule_of_law.notes: Pervasive corruption, militia influence over institutions, and weak courts. (WJP 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak, opaque, and capacity-limited. (IMF; World Bank)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Bloated and patronage-ridden under the muhasasa quota system, with weak delivery. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A poor RSF standing, with journalist intimidation and militia threats. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: A fragile sectarian system, restricted liberties, and deep cleavages, with a young-population strain.

political_stability.notes: A fragile sectarian power-sharing system with Iran-aligned militia leverage and recurrent government-formation crises, though calmer than the ISIS-era. (ICG; Al-Monitor)

civil_liberties.notes: Restricted, with militia/morality pressures and a 2024 law criminalising same-sex relations. (HRW; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited routes, not a relocation destination, hosting and returning refugees and IDPs. (UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep Shia-Sunni-Kurd cleavages and unresolved Baghdad-Kurdistan disputes. (ICG; Rudaw)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing - an acute jobs strain now, a potential later dividend. (World Bank; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A contested US-Iran arena with residual insurgency and dollar-flow restrictions.

alliance_reliability.notes: A contested US-Iran arena - hedged and unreliable, with sovereignty constrained by armed factions. (ICG; CSIS)

conflict_involvement.notes: Residual jihadist insurgency, militia attacks, US/Iran/Israel spillover, and Kurdistan tensions. (ISW; press)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Dollar-flow restrictions and sanctioned-bank exclusions tied to Iran-sanctions enforcement. (US Treasury; Iraqi Oil Report)

physical_practical.notes: A severe water/heat crisis and electricity failure amid oil wealth.

climate.notes: Severe - extreme heat near 50C, an acute Tigris/Euphrates water crisis worsened by upstream dams, desertification, and sandstorms. (UN; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Chronic electricity shortages despite oil wealth, with dependence on Iranian power. (Iraqi Oil Report; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Improved from the war era, with residual militia/kidnapping risk. (press)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration against high hazard. (industry context)

summary: A clearly-negative oil rentier: roughly 90% of state revenue comes from oil, with almost no diversification, a bloated payroll, a weak cash-based banking sector, and a managed dinar dogged by dollar-access frictions tied to Iran-sanctions enforcement. Institutions are corruption- and militia-captured under a fragile sectarian power-sharing system caught between the US and Iran, with residual insurgency and Baghdad-Kurdistan disputes. The standout physical drags are a severe water and extreme-heat crisis and chronic electricity failure despite the oil wealth.
